topic: A person finds their spouse's secret savings account
raw_output:
[story 1]

The envelope felt heavy in my hand, the paper thick and official, a stark contrast to the casualness of the rest of the mail piled on my desk. Inside, a bank statement, neatly printed, detailed transactions I hadn't known existed. My jaw tightened, a muscle jumping near my temple. I stared at the figures, the numbers swimming before my eyes, each one a sharp, unwelcome intrusion into the carefully constructed reality I thought we shared. A cold knot formed in my stomach, tightening with every line item. I slowly folded the paper, the sound crisp in the quiet apartment, and placed it on the counter, refusing to look at the contents again. The silence in the room amplified the frantic thumping of my own pulse.

[story 2]

I found the slip tucked inside the lining of his old winter coat. It was tucked away, hidden, a small betrayal unearthed from the mundane. My hands shook slightly as I unfolded the paper, the ink seeming to mock me with its undeniable truth. Every transaction listed was a silent accusation. A wave of intense irritation washed over me, making my skin feel tight. I reread the transfer amounts, my breath catching in my throat. I wanted to slam the paper down, to shatter the evidence, but my fingers remained frozen around the brittle edges. I stood by the window, watching the streetlights blur, feeling a burning resentment rise in my chest, a suffocating pressure that made breathing shallow.

[story 3]

It was the sheer audacity of it that truly grated on me. The discovery wasn't just the money; it was the clandestine nature of it, the deliberate concealment. I picked up the document, the texture rough under my fingertips, and felt a sudden, sharp surge of anger. My gaze swept over the details, cataloging the hidden life that existed parallel to the life we presented. A deep, unpleasant churning started low in my gut. I crumpled the paper once, then smoothed it out again, my movements jerky and precise. When I finally put it down, the air felt charged, thick with unspoken disapproval. I turned away, focusing intently on a chipped corner of the wall, trying to quell the rising tide of displeasure that threatened to overwhelm me.
